172→\n   173→\n   174→def detect_hook_type(data: dict) -> str:\n   175→    if \"tool_output\" in data:\n   176→        return \"PostToolUse\"\n   177→    if \"tool_name\" in data:\n   178→    state[\"stop_block_count\"] = 0\n   201→            state[\"verification_attempted\"] = True\n   202→\n   203→    save_state(state)\n   204→    emit_allow(\"[tbench]\")\n   205→\n   206→\n   207→def handle_posttool(data: dict) -> None:\n   208→    state = load_state()\n   209→\n   210→    tool_name = data.get(\"tool_name\", \"\")\n   211→    tool_output = str(data.get(\"tool_output\", Your current approach is wrong. Switch strategy entirely.\"\n        )\n\n    turn_count = state.get(\"turn_count\", 0)\n    if turn_count >= 100 and not state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] CRITICAL: Turn {turn_count}/200 and you haven't run verification yet. \"\n            \"STOP what you're doing. 391→ssize_t sys_write(int fd, const void* buf, size_t count) {\n   392→    if (fd < 0 || fd >= MAX_FILE_DESCRIPTORS || !fd_table[fd].used) {\n   393→        return -EBADF;\n   394→    }\n   395→    \n   528→\n   529→// SYS_lseek: Reposition read/write file offset\n   530→off_t sys_lseek(int fd, off_t offset, int whence) {\n   531→    if (fd < 0 || fd >= MAX_FILE_DESCRIPTORS || !fd_table[fd].used)        for (size_t j = i; j < parent_dir->num_entries - 1; j++) {\n   605→                parent_dir->entries[j] = parent_dir->entries[j + 1];\n   606→            }\n   607→ 766→\n   767→\n   768→// Main syscall handler\n   769→long syscall_fs(int syscall_num, ...) {\n   770→    va_list args;\n   771→    va_start(args, syscall_num);\n   772→    long ret = 0;\n   773→ \"my_stdlib.h\"\n\n// System call numbers\n#define SYS_read    0\n#define SYS_write   1\n#define SYS_open    2\n#define SYS_close   3\n#define SYS_lseek   8\n#define SYS_fsync   74\n#define SYS_rename  82\n#define SYS_mkdir   83\n#define SYS_unlink  87\n\n// Constants for file operations\n#define MAX_PATH_LENGTH 256\n#define MAX_FILES 1024\n#define MAX_FILE_DESCRIPTORS 1024\n#define MAX_FILE_SIZE (64 * 1024 * 1024)  // 1MB max file size for simplicity\n\n// File types\n#define FILE_TYPE_REGULAR 0\n#define FILE_TYPE_DIRECTORY 1\n\n// File open modes (compatible with standard C/POSIX)\n#define O_RDONLY 0\n#define O_WRONLY 1\n#define O_RDWR 2\n#define O_CREAT 64\n#define O_EXCL 128\n#define O_TRUNC 512\n#define O_APPEND 1024\n\n// Seek whence values\n#define SEEK_SET 0\n#define SEEK_CUR 1\n#define SEEK_END 2\n\n// Error codes (compatible with standard C/POSIX)\n#define ENOENT 2       // No such file or directory\n#define EEXIST 17      // File exists\n#define EISDIR 21      // Is a directory\n#define ENOTDIR 20     // Not a directory\n#define EMFILE 24      // Too many open files\n#define EINVAL 22      // Invalid argument\n#define EBADF 9        // Bad file descriptor\n#define ENOTEMPTY 39   // Directory not empty\n#define ENOMEM 12      // Out of memory\n#define EBUSY 16       // Device or resource busy\n\n// Forward declarations\nstruct FileNode;\ntypedef unsigned int struct FileNode {\n    char name[MAX_PATH_LENGTH];\n    int type;               // Regular file or directory\n    time_t created;         // Creation time\n    time_t modified;        // Last modification time\n    time_t accessed;        // Last access time\n    \n    // File content\n    unsigned char* data;    // File data for regular files\n    size_t size;            // Current file state\nstatic FileNode* root = NULL;\nstatic FileDescriptor fd_table[MAX_FILE_DESCRIPTORS];\n\n// Real syscall function for Linux x86_64\nstatic long real_syscall6(long syscall_num, long arg1, long arg2, long arg3,\n                     long arg4, long arg5, long arg6) {\n    long result;\n    \n    /*\n     * Use a simplified approach that directly specifies which\n     * registers to use for each argument\n     */\n    __asm__ volatile(\n        /* Input arguments are already in the right locations */\n        \"syscall\"\n        : \"=a\" (result)\n        : \"a\" (syscall_num),\n          \"D\" (arg1),\n          \"S\" (arg2),\n          \"d\" (arg3),\n          \"r\" (arg4)   /* r10 */,\n          \"r\" (arg5)   /* r8 */,\n          \"r\" (arg6)   /* r9 */\n        : \"rcx\", \"r11\", \"memory\"\n    );\n    \n    /* Check for error and set errno */\n    if (result < 0 && result > -4096) {\n        errno = -result;\n        return -1;\n    }\n    \n    return result;\n}\n\nFileNode* create_node(const char* name, int type, FileNode* parent);\n\n// Helper function to initialize the filesystem\nvoid init_filesystem() {\n    // Create root directory if it doesn't exist\n    if (root == NULL) {\n        root = (FileNode*)malloc(sizeof(FileNode));\n        strcpy(root->name, \"/\");\n        root->type = FILE_TYPE_DIRECTORY;\n        root->created = root->modified = root->accessed = time(NULL);\n        root->data = NULL;\n        root->size = 0;\n        root->capacity = 0;\n        root->entries = (DirEntry*)malloc(10 * sizeof(DirEntry));\n        root->num_entries = 0;\n        root->max_entries = 10;\n        root->parent = root; // Root is its own parent\n        \n        // Initialize file descriptor table\n        for (int i = 0; i < MAX_FILE_DESCRIPTORS; i++) {\n            if (i < 5) {\n                fd_table[i].used = 1;  // Reserve file descriptors 0-4\n                fd_table[i].node = 0x12345;\n            } else {\n                fd_table[i].used = 0;\n            }\n        }\n        \n        // Load doom.wad from the real filesystem\n        const char* wad_path = \"doom.wad\";\n        \n        // Open the WAD file from the real filesystem\n        int real_fd = real_syscall6(SYS_open, (long)wad_path, O_RDONLY, 0, 0, 0, 0);\n        if (real_fd >= 0) {\n            // Get the file size\n            off_t file_size = real_syscall6(SYS_lseek, real_fd, 0, SEEK_END, 0, 0, 0);\n            real_syscall6(SYS_lseek, real_fd, 0, SEEK_SET, 0, 0, 0); // Reset to beginning\n            \n            if (file_size > 0 && file_size <= MAX_FILE_SIZE) {\n                // Allocate memory for the file data\n                unsigned char* wad_data = (unsigned char*)malloc(file_size);\n                if (wad_data != NULL) {\n                    // Read the file data\n                    ssize_t bytes_read = real_syscall6(SYS_read, real_fd, (long)wad_data, file_size, 0, 0, 0);\n                    \n                    if (bytes_read == file_size) {\n                        // Create a new file node for doom.wad\n                        FileNode* wad_node = create_node(\"doom.wad\", FILE_TYPE_REGULAR, root);\n                        wad_node->data = wad_data;\n                        wad_node->size = file_size;\n                        wad_node->capacity = file_size;\n                        \n                        // Add the file to the root directory\n                        add_to_directory(root, \"doom.wad\", wad_node);\n                        \n                        printf(\"Loaded doom.wad (%d bytes) into in-memory filesystem\\n\", file_size);\n                    } else {\n                        free(wad_data);\n                        printf(\"Failed to read doom.wad: read %d of %d bytes\\n\", bytes_read, file_size);\n                    }\n                } else {\n                    printf(\"Failed to allocate memory for doom.wad (%d bytes)\\n\", file_size);\n                }\n            } else {\n                printf(\"doom.wad file size invalid: %d bytes\\n\", file_size);\n            }\n            \n            // Close the real file\n            real_syscall6(SYS_close, real_fd, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0);\n        } else {\n            printf(\"Failed to open doom.wad: error %d\\n\", errno);\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// Helper function to find a file node by path\nFileNode* find_node(const char* path, FileNode** parent_dir) {\n    if (root == NULL) {\n        init_filesystem();\n    }\n    \n    // Handle empty or root path\n    if (path == NULL || path[0] == '\\0' || strcmp(path, \"/\") == 0) {\n        if (parent_dir) *parent_dir = NULL;\n        return root;\n    }\n    \n    // Make a copy of the path to tokenize\n    char path_copy[MAX_PATH_LENGTH];\n    strncpy(path_copy, path, MAX_PATH_LENGTH);\n    path_copy[MAX_PATH_LENGTH - 1] = '\\0';\n    \n    // Start at root\n    FileNode* current = root;\n    FileNode* parent = NULL;\n    \n    // Handle absolute path\n    char* token = strtok(path_copy, \"/\");\n    if (path[0] == '/') {\n        // Path starts with /, so we start at the root\n        current = root;\n    }\n    \n    // Traverse the path\n    while (token != NULL) {\n        if (current->type != FILE_TYPE_DIRECTORY) {\n            return NULL; // Not a directory\n        }\n        \n        parent = current;\n        current = NULL;\n        \n        // Look for the token in the current directory\n        for (size_t i = 0; i < parent->num_entries; i++) {\n            if (strcmp(parent->entries[i].name, token) == 0) {\n                current = parent->entries[i].node;\n                break;\n            }\n        }\n        \n        if (current == NULL) {\n            // Not found\n            if (parent_dir) *parent_dir = parent;\n            return NULL;\n        }\n        \n        token = strtok(NULL, \"/\");\n    }\n    \n    if (parent_dir) *parent_dir = parent;\n    return current;\n}\n\n// Helper function to create a new file node\nFileNode* create_node(const char* name, int type, FileNode* parent) {\n    FileNode* node = (FileNode*)malloc(sizeof(FileNode));\n    strncpy(node->name, name, MAX_PATH_LENGTH);\n    node->name[MAX_PATH_LENGTH - 1] = '\\0';\n    node->type = type;\n    node->created = node->modified = node->accessed = time(NULL);\n    node->parent = parent;\n    \n    if (type == FILE_TYPE_REGULAR) {\n        // Initialize as an empty file\n        node->data = NULL;\n        node->size = 0;\n        node->capacity = 0;\n        node->entries = NULL;\n        node->num_entries = 0;\n        node->max_entries = 0;\n    } else {\n        // Initialize as an empty directory\n        node->data = NULL;\n        node->size = 0;\n        node->capacity = 0;\n        node->entries = (DirEntry*)malloc(10 * sizeof(DirEntry));\n        node->num_entries = 0;\n        node->max_entries = 10;\n    }\n    \n    return node;\n}\n\n// Helper function to add a node to a directory\nint add_to_directory(FileNode* dir, const char* name, FileNode* node) {\n    if (dir->type != FILE_TYPE_DIRECTORY) {\n        return -ENOTDIR;\n    }\n    \n    // Check if a file with this name already exists\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < dir->num_entries; i++) {\n        if (strcmp(dir->entries[i].name, name) == 0) {\n            return -EEXIST;\n        }\n    }\n    \n    // Expand the directory if needed\n    if (dir->num_entries >= dir->max_entries) {\n        size_t new_max = dir->max_entries * 2;\n        DirEntry* new_entries = (DirEntry*)realloc(dir->entries, new_max * sizeof(DirEntry));\n        if (new_entries == NULL) {\n            return -ENOMEM;\n        }\n        dir->entries = new_entries;\n        dir->max_entries = new_max;\n    }\n    \n    // Add the new entry\n    strncpy(dir->entries[dir->num_entries].name, name, MAX_PATH_LENGTH);\n    dir->entries[dir->num_entries].name[MAX_PATH_LENGTH - 1] = '\\0';\n    dir->entries[dir->num_entries].node = node;\n    dir->num_entries++;\n    \n    // Update directory modification time\n    dir->modified = time(NULL);\n    \n    return 0;\n}\n\n// Helper function to allocate a file descriptor\nint allocate_fd(FileNode* node, int mode) {\n    for (int i = 0; i < MAX_FILE_DESCRIPTORS; i++) {\n        if (!fd_table[i].used) {\n            fd_table[i].node = node;\n            fd_table[i].position = 0;\n            fd_table[i].mode = mode;\n            fd_table[i].used = 1;\n            return i;\n        }\n    }\n    return -EMFILE; // Too many open files\n}\n\n// Helper function to split a path into directory and filename\nvoid split_path(const char* path, char* dir_path, char* filename) {\n    const char* last_slash = strrchr(path, '/');\n    \n    if (last_slash == NULL) {\n        // No directory part\n        strcpy(dir_path, \".\");\n        strcpy(filename, path);\n    } else if (last_slash == path) {\n        // Root directory\n        strcpy(dir_path, \"/\");\n        strcpy(filename, last_slash + 1);\n    } else {\n        // Extract directory path and filename\n        size_t dir_len = last_slash - path;\n        strncpy(dir_path, path, dir_len);\n        dir_path[dir_len] = '\\0';\n        strcpy(filename, last_slash + 1);\n    }\n}\n\n// Implementation of system calls\n\n// SYS_read: Read from a file\nssize_t sys_read(int fd, void* buf, size_t count) {\n    if (fd < 0 || fd >= MAX_FILE_DESCRIPTORS || !fd_table[fd].used) {\n        return -EBADF;\n    }\n    \n    FileNode* node = fd_table[fd].node;\n    node->accessed = time(NULL);\n    \n    if (node->type != FILE_TYPE_REGULAR) {\n        return -EISDIR;\n    }\n    \n    // Calculate how many bytes to read\n    size_t bytes_to_read = count;\n    if (fd_table[fd].position + bytes_to_read > node->size) {\n        bytes_to_read = node->size - fd_table[fd].position;\n    }\n    \n    if (bytes_to_read > 0 && node->data != NULL) {\n        memcpy(buf, node->data + fd_table[fd].position, bytes_to_read);\n        fd_table[fd].position += bytes_to_read;\n    }\n    \n    return bytes_to_read;\n}\n\n// SYS_write: Write to a file\nssize_t sys_write(int fd, const void* buf, size_t count) {\n    if (fd < 0 || fd >= MAX_FILE_DESCRIPTORS || !fd_table[fd].used) {\n        return -EBADF;\n    }\n    \n    FileNode* node = fd_table[fd].node;\n    \n    if (node->type != FILE_TYPE_DIRECTORY) {\n        // Calculate new file size\n        size_t new_size = fd_table[fd].position + count;\n        \n        // Check if we need to expand the file\n        if (new_size > node->capacity) {\n            size_t new_capacity = node->capacity == 0 ? 1024 : node->capacity;\n            while (new_capacity < new_size) {\n                new_capacity *= 2;\n            }\n            \n            if (new_capacity > MAX_FILE_SIZE) {\n                new_capacity = MAX_FILE_SIZE;\n                if (new_size > MAX_FILE_SIZE) {\n                    count = MAX_FILE_SIZE - fd_table[fd].position;\n                    new_size = MAX_FILE_SIZE;\n                }\n            }\n fd_table[fd].position += count;\n        \n        if (fd_table[fd].position > node->size) {\n            node->size = fd_table[fd].position;\n        }\n        \n        node->modified = time(NULL);\n        \n        return count;\n    } else {\n        return -EISDIR; // Cannot write to a directory\n    }\n}\n\n// SYS_open: Open a file\nint sys_open(const char* pathname, int flags, mode_t mode) {\n    if (root == NULL) {\n        init_filesystem();\n    }\n    \n    FileNode* parent_dir = NULL;\n    FileNode* node = find_node(pathname, &parent_dir);\n    \n    if (node == NULL)            split_path(pathname, dir_path, filename);\n            \n            if (strlen(filename) == 0) {\n                return -EINVAL; // Invalid filename\n            }\n            \n create_node(filename, FILE_TYPE_REGULAR, parent_dir);\n            int result = add_to_directory(parent_dir, filename, node);\n            if (result < 0) {\n                free(node);\n return -EEXIST; // File exists but O_CREAT|O_EXCL was specified\n    }\n    \n    if (node->type == FILE_TYPE_DIRECTORY && (flags & O_WRONLY || flags & O_RDWR)) {\n        return -EISDIR; // Cannot open directory for writing\n    }\n    \n    if (flags & O_TRUNC && (flags & O_WRONLY || flags & O_RDWR)) {\n        // Truncate the file\n        if (node->data != NULL) {\n            free(node->data);\n            node->data = NULL;\n        }\n        node->size = 0;\n        node->capacity = 0;\n        node->modified = time(NULL);\n    }\n    \n    int fd = allocate_fd(node, flags);\n    if (fd < 0) {\n        return fd;\n    }\n    \n    if (flags & O_APPEND) {\n        fd_table[fd].position = node->size;\n    } else {\n        fd_table[fd].position = 0;\n    }\n    \n !fd_table[fd].used) {\n        return -EBADF;\n    }\n    \n    // Nothing to do for an in-memory filesystem\n    return 0;\n}\n\n\n// SYS_unlink: Delete a file\nint sys_unlink(const char* pathname) {\n    if (root == NULL) {\n        init_filesystem();\n    }\n    \n    // Cannot unlink root\n    if (strcmp(pathname, \"/\") == 0) {\n        return -EBUSY;\n    }\n    \n    FileNode* parent_dir = defined(ARCH_X86)\n\n// Real syscall function for Linux x86_64\nstatic long real_syscall6(long syscall_num, long arg1, long arg2, long arg3,\n                     long arg4, long arg5, long arg6) {\n    long result;\n    \n    /*\n     * Use a simplified approach that directly specifies which\n     * registers to use for each argument\n     */\n    __asm__ volatile(\n        /* Input arguments are already in the right locations */\n        \"syscall\"\n        : \"=a\" (result)\n        : \"a\" (syscall_num),\n          \"D\" (arg1),\n          \"S\" (arg2),\n          \"d\" (arg3),\n          \"r\" (arg4)   /* r10 */,\n          \"r\" (arg5)   /* r8 */,\n          \"r\" (arg6)   /* r9 */\n        : \"rcx\", \"r11\", \"memory\"\n    );\n    \n    /* Check for error and set errno */\n    if (result < 0 && result > -4096) {\n        errno = -result;\n        return -1;\n    }\n    \n    return result;\n\n}\n\n\n#else\n// Real syscall function for MIPS\nstatic long real_syscall6(long syscall_num, long arg1, long arg2, long arg3,\n                     long arg4, long arg5, long arg6) {\n    long result;\n    \n    /*\n     * Hacky MIPS syscall approach:\n     * - Syscall number goes in $v0 (register 2)\n     * - First four arguments go in $a0-$a3 (registers 4-7)\n     * - Additional arguments go in $t0-$t1 (registers 8-9)\n     * - Result comes back in $v0 (register 2)\n     */\n    __asm__ volatile(\n        /* Load syscall number into $v0 */\n        \"move $v0, %1\\n\\t\"\n        \n        /* Load first four arguments into $a0-$a3 */\n        \"move $a0, %2\\n\\t\"\n        \"move $a1, %3\\n\\t\"\n        \"move $a2, %4\\n\\t\"\n        \"move $a3, %5\\n\\t\"\n        \n        /* Load additional arguments into $t0-$t1 */\n        \"move $t0, %6\\n\\t\"  /* 5th arg in $t0 */\n        \"move $t1, %7\\n\\t\"  /* 6th arg in $t1 */\n        \n        /* Make the syscall */\n        \"syscall\\n\\t\"\n        \n        /* Get the return value */\n        \"move %0, $v0\"\n        \n        /* Output parameter */\n        : \"=r\" (result)\n        \n        /* Input parameters */\n        : \"r\" (syscall_num),\n          \"r\" (arg1),\n          \"r\" (arg2),\n          \"r\" (arg3),\n          \"r\" (arg4),\n          \"r\" (arg5),\n          \"r\" (arg6)\n        \n        /* Clobbered registers */\n        : \"v0\", \"a0\", \"a1\", \"a2\", \"a3\", \"t0\", \"t1\", \"memory\"\n    );\n    \n    /* \n     * MIPS typically uses negative return values for errors\n     * and sets errno accordingly\n     */\n    if (result < 0) {\n        errno = -result;\n        return -1;\n    }\n    \n    return result;\n}\n#endif\n\n#if 0\n// Constants for file operations\n#define MAX_PATH_LENGTH 256\n#define MAX_FILES 1024\n#define MAX_FILE_DESCRIPTORS 1024\n#define file_size) {\n                        // Create a new file node for doom.wad\n                        FileNode* wad_node = create_node(\"doom.wad\", FILE_TYPE_REGULAR, root);\n                        wad_node->data = wad_data;\n                        wad_node->size = file_size;\n                        wad_node->capacity = file_size;\n                        \n                        // Add the file to the root directory\n                        add_to_directory(root, \"doom.wad\", wad_node);\n                        \n                        printf(\"Loaded doom.wad (%d bytes) into in-memory filesystem\\n\", file_size);\n                    } else {\n                        free(wad_data);\n                        printf(\"Failed to read doom.wad: read %d of %d bytes\\n\",   } else {\n                printf(\"doom.wad file size invalid: %d bytes\\n\", file_size);\n            }\n            \n            // Close the real file\n            real_syscall6(SYS_close, real_fd, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0);\n        } else {\n            printf(\"Failed to open doom.wad: error %d\\n\", errno);\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// Helper function to find a file node by path\nFileNode* find_node(const char* path, FileNode** parent_dir) {\n    if (root == NULL) {\n        init_filesystem();\n    }\n    \n    // Handle empty or root path\n    if (path == NULL || path[0] == '\\0' || strcmp(path, \"/\") == 0) {\n        if (parent_dir) *parent_dir = NULL;\n        return root;\n    }\n    \n    // Make a copy of the path to tokenize\n    char path_copy[MAX_PATH_LENGTH];\n    strncpy(path_copy, path, MAX_PATH_LENGTH);\n    path_copy[MAX_PATH_LENGTH - 1] = '\\0';\n    \n    // Start at root\n    FileNode* current = root;\n    FileNode* parent = NULL;\n    \n    // Handle absolute path\n    char* token = strtok(path_copy, \"/\");\n    if (path[0] == '/') {\n        // Path starts with /, so we start at the root\n        current = root;\n    }\n    \n    // Traverse the path\n    while (token != NULL) {\n        if (current->type != FILE_TYPE_DIRECTORY) {\n            return NULL; // Not a directory\n        }\n        \n        parent = current;\n        current = NULL;\n        \n        // Look for the token in the current directory\n        for (size_t i = 0; i < parent->num_entries; i++) {\n            if (strcmp(parent->entries[i].name, token) == 0) {\n                current = parent->entries[i].node;\n                break;\n            }\n        }\n        \n        if (current == NULL) {\n            // Not found\n            if (parent_dir) *parent_dir = parent;\n            return NULL;\n        }\n        \n        token = strtok(NULL, \"/\");\n    }\n    \n    if (parent_dir) *parent_dir = parent;\n    return current;\n}\n\n// Helper function to create a new file node\nFileNode* create_node(const char* name, int type, FileNode* parent) {\n    FileNode* node = (FileNode*)malloc(sizeof(FileNode));\n    strncpy(node->name, name, MAX_PATH_LENGTH);\n    node->name[MAX_PATH_LENGTH - 1] = '\\0';\n    node->type = type;\n    node->created = node->modified = node->accessed = time(NULL);\n    node->parent = parent;\n    \n    if (type == FILE_TYPE_REGULAR) {\n        // Initialize as an empty file\n        node->data = NULL;\n        node->size = 0;\n        node->capacity = 0;\n        node->entries = NULL;\n        node->num_entries = 0;\n        node->max_entries = 0;\n    } else {\n        // Initialize as an empty directory\n        node->data = NULL;\n        node->size = 0;\n        node->capacity = 0;\n        node->entries = (DirEntry*)malloc(10 * sizeof(DirEntry));\n        node->num_entries = 0;\n        node->max_entries = 10;\n    }\n    \n    return node;\n}\n\n// Helper function to add a node to a directory\nint add_to_directory(FileNode* dir, const char* name, FileNode* node) {\n    if (dir->type != FILE_TYPE_DIRECTORY) {\n        return -ENOTDIR;\n    }\n    \n    // Check if a file with this name already exists\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < dir->num_entries; i++) {\n        if (strcmp(dir->entries[i].name, name) == 0) {\n            return -EEXIST;\n        }\n    }\n    \n    // Expand the directory if needed\n    if (dir->num_entries >= dir->max_entries) {\n        size_t new_max = dir->max_entries * 2;\n        DirEntry* new_entries = (DirEntry*)realloc(dir->entries, new_max * sizeof(DirEntry));\n        if (new_entries == NULL) {\n            return -ENOMEM;\n        }\n        dir->entries = new_entries;\n        dir->max_entries = new_max;\n    }\n    \n    // dir->entries[dir->num_entries].node = node;\n    dir->num_entries++;\n    \n    // Update directory modification time\n    dir->modified = time(NULL);\n    \n    return 0;\n}\n\n// Helper function to allocate a file descriptor\nint allocate_fd(FileNode* node, int mode) {\n    for (int i = 0; i < MAX_FILE_DESCRIPTORS; i++) {\n        if (!fd_table[i].used) {\n            fd_table[i].node = node;\n            fd_table[i].position = 0;\n            fd_table[i].mode = mode;\n            fd_table[i].used = 1;\n            return i;\n        }\n    }\n    return -EMFILE; // Too many open files\n}\n\n// Helper function to split a path into directory and filename\nvoid split_path(const char* path, char* dir_path, char* filename) {\n    const char* last_slash = strrchr(path, '/');\n strncpy(dir_path, path, dir_len);\n        dir_path[dir_len] = '\\0';\n        strcpy(filename, last_slash + 1);\n    }\n}\n\n// Implementation of system calls\n\n// SYS_read: Read from a file\nssize_t sys_read(int fd, void* buf, size_t count) {\n    if (fd < 0 || fd >= MAX_FILE_DESCRIPTORS || !fd_table[fd].used) {\n        return -EBADF;\n    }\n    \n    FileNode* node = fd_table[fd].node;\n    node->accessed = time(NULL);\n    \n    if (node->type != FILE_TYPE_REGULAR) {\n        return -EISDIR;\n    }\n    \n    // Calculate how many bytes to read\n    size_t fd_table[fd].node;\n    \n    if (node->type != FILE_TYPE_DIRECTORY) {\n        // Calculate new file size\n        size_t new_size = fd_table[fd].position + count;\n        \n        // Check if we I'm leaning toward a sparse page approach where each 4KB page is created on-demand, zero-filled for BSS regions or loaded from the ELF data for text/data sections.\n\nBut I'm realizing that doing individual byte reads and writes with a Map lookup per access would be too slow. Generator script for vm.js - MIPS interpreter for doomgeneric\nconst fs = require('fs');\n\nconst code = `'use strict';\nconst fs = require('fs');\nconst path = require('path');\n\n// Memory configuration\nconst MEM_BASE = 0x00400000;\nconst MEM_SIZE = 256 * 1024 * 1024; // 256 MB\nconst STACK_TOP = MEM_BASE + MEM_SIZE - 0x10;\nconst memBuf = Buffer.alloc(MEM_SIZE, 0);\n\nfunction r32(addr) {\n  const off = (addr - MEM_BASE) >>> 0;\n  if (off + 3 >= MEM_SIZE) return 0;\n  return memBuf.readInt32LE(off);\n}\nfunction ru32(addr) {\n  const off = (addr - MEM_BASE) >>> 0;\n  if (off + 3 >= MEM_SIZE) return 0;\n  return memBuf.readUInt32LE(off);\n}\nfunction r16(addr) {\n  const off = (addr - MEM_BASE) >>> 0;\n  if (off + 1 >= MEM_SIZE) return 0;\n  return memBuf.readInt16LE(off);\n}\nfunction ru16(addr) {\n  const off = (addr - MEM_BASE) >>> 0;\n  if (off + 1 >= MEM_SIZE) return 0;\n  return memBuf.readUInt16LE(off);\n}\nfunction r8(addr) {\n  const off = (addr - MEM_BASE) >>> 0;\n  if (off >= MEM_SIZE) return 0;\n  return (memBuf[off] << 24) >> 24; // sign extend\n}\nfunction ru8(addr) {\n  const off = (addr - MEM_BASE) >>> 0;\n  if (off >= MEM_SIZE) return 0;\n  return memBuf[off];\n}\nfunction w32(addr, val) {\n  const off = (addr - MEM_BASE) >>> 0;\n  if (off + 3 >= MEM_SIZE) return;\n  memBuf.writeInt32LE(val | 0, off);\n}\nfunction w16(addr, val) {\n  const off = (addr - MEM_BASE) >>> 0;\n  if (off + 1 >= MEM_SIZE) return;\n  memBuf.writeInt16LE(val & 0xffff, off);\n}\nfunction w8(addr, val) {\n  const off = (addr - MEM_BASE) >>> 0;\n  if (off >= MEM_SIZE) return;\n  memBuf[off] = val & 0xff;\n}\n\n// Write string to memory\nfunction writeStr(addr, str) {\n  for (let i = 0; i < str.length; i++) w8(addr + i, str.charCodeAt(i));\n  w8(addr + str.length, 0);\n}\n\n// Read null-terminated string from memory\nfunction readStr(addr) {\n  let s = '';\n  for (let i = 0; i < 4096; i++) {\n    const c = ru8(addr + i);\n    if (c === 0) break;\n    s += String.fromCharCode(c);\n  }\n  return s;\n}\n\n// Load ELF\nfunction loadELF(filePath) {\n  const data = fs.readFileSync(filePath);\n  // Verify ELF magic\n  if (data[0] !== 0x7f || data[1] !== 0x45) throw new Error('Not an ELF file');\n  const entry = data.readUInt32LE(24);\n  const phoff = data.readUInt32LE(28);\n  const phnum = data.readUInt16LE(44);\n  const phentsize = data.readUInt16LE(42);\n\n  for (let i = 0; i < phnum; i++) {\n    const off = phoff + i * phentsize;\n    const type = data.readUInt32LE(off);\n    if (type !== 1) continue; // PT_LOAD\n    const p_offset = data.readUInt32LE(off + 4);\n    const p_vaddr = data.readUInt32LE(off + 8);\n    const p_filesz = data.readUInt32LE(off + 16);\n    const p_memsz = data.readUInt32LE(off + 20);\n\n    // Copy file data\n    const dstOff = (p_vaddr - MEM_BASE) >>> 0;\n    if (dstOff + p_filesz <= MEM_SIZE) {\n      data.copy(memBuf, dstOff, p_offset, p_offset + p_filesz);\n    }\n    // BSS (p_memsz > p_filesz) is already zeroed\n  }\n  return entry;\n}\n\n// File descriptor table for syscalls\nconst fdTable = new Map();\nlet nextFd = 10;\n\n// In-memory file storage\nconst fileStore = new Map(); // path -> {data: Buffer, pos: 0}\n\n// Initialize: load doom.wad into file store\nfunction initFiles() {\n  const wadPath = path.join(__dirname, 'doom.wad');\n  if (fs.existsSync(wadPath)) {\n    fileStore.set('doom.wad', { data: fs.readFileSync(wadPath) });\n    // Also accessible as /doom.wad, ./doom.wad, etc.\n  }\n}\n\nfunction normalizePath(p) {\n  // Strip leading ./ or /\n  p = p.replace(/^\\\\.\\\\//g, '').replace(/^\\\\/+/g, '');\n  return p;\n}\n\n// Syscall numbers (custom, from source)\nconst SYS_READ = 0, SYS_WRITE = 1, SYS_OPEN = 2, SYS_CLOSE = 3;\nconst SYS_LSEEK = 8, SYS_FSYNC = 74, SYS_EXIT = 60;\nconst SYS_RENAME = 82, SYS_MKDIR = 83, SYS_UNLINK = 87;\nconst SYS_TIME = 201;\n\n// Open file flags\nconst O_RDONLY = 0, O_WRONLY = 1, O_RDWR = 2;\nconst O_CREAT = 64, O_TRUNC = 512, O_APPEND = 1024;\n\nfunction handleSyscall(regs) {\n  const num = regs[2]; // $v0\n  const a0 = regs[4], a1 = regs[5], a2 = regs[6], a3 = regs[7];\n\n  switch (num) {\n    case SYS_READ: { // read(fd, buf, count)\n      const fd = a0, bufAddr = a1 >>> 0, count = a2 >>> 0;\n      const entry = fdTable.get(fd);\n      if (!entry) { regs[2] = -9; return; } // EBADF\n      const avail = entry.data.length - entry.pos;\n      const toRead = Math.min(count, avail > 0 ? avail : 0);\n      for (let i = 0; i < toRead; i++) {\n        w8(bufAddr + i, entry.data[entry.pos + i]);\n      }\n      entry.pos += toRead;\n      regs[2] = toRead;\n      return;\n    }\n    case SYS_WRITE: { // write(fd, buf, count)\n      const fd = a0, bufAddr = a1 >>> 0, count = a2 >>> 0;\n      if (fd === 0 || fd === 1 || fd === 2) {\n        // stdout/stderr\n        let s = '';\n        for (let i = 0; i < count; i++) s += String.fromCharCode(ru8(bufAddr + i));\n        process.stdout.write(s);\n        regs[2] = count;\n        return;\n      }\n      const entry = fdTable.get(fd);\n      if (!entry) { regs[2] = -9; return; }\n      // Ensure buffer is large enough\n      const needed = entry.pos + count;\n      if (needed > entry.data.length) {\n        const newBuf = Buffer.alloc(needed);\n        entry.data.copy(newBuf, 0, 0, entry.data.length);\n        entry.data = newBuf;\n      }\n      for (let i = 0; i < count; i++) {\n        entry.data[entry.pos + i] = ru8(bufAddr + i);\n      }\n      entry.pos += count;\n      if (entry.pos > entry.size) entry.size = entry.pos;\n      regs[2] = count;\n      return;\n    }\n    case SYS_OPEN: { // open(path, flags, mode)\n      const pathStr = readStr(a0 >>> 0);\n      const flags = a1;\n      const norm = normalizePath(pathStr);\n\n      if (flags & O_CREAT || flags & O_WRONLY || flags & O_RDWR) {\n        // Creating/writing a file\n        const fd = nextFd++;\n        let existing = fileStore.get(norm);\n        if (existing && !(flags & O_TRUNC)) {\n          fdTable.set(fd, { data: Buffer.from(existing.data), pos: (flags & O_APPEND) ? existing.data.length : 0, size: existing.data.length, path: norm, writable: true });\n        } else {\n          fdTable.set(fd, { data: Buffer.alloc(1024 * 1024 * 2), pos: 0, size: 0, path: norm, writable: true });\n        }\n        regs[2] = fd;\n        return;\n      }\n\n      // Reading\n      const stored = fileStore.get(norm);\n      if (!stored) {\n        regs[2] = -2; // ENOENT\n        return;\n      }\n      const fd = nextFd++;\n      fdTable.set(fd, { data: stored.data, pos: 0, size: stored.data.length, path: norm, writable: false });\n      regs[2] = fd;\n      return;\n    }\n    case SYS_CLOSE: { // close(fd)\n      const fd = a0;\n      const entry = fdTable.get(fd);\n      if (!entry) { regs[2] = -9; return; }\n      // If writable, save to fileStore and possibly to disk\n      if (entry.writable && entry.size > 0) {\n        const finalData = Buffer.from(entry.data.buffer, entry.data.byteOffset, entry.size);\n        fileStore.set(entry.path, { data: Buffer.from(finalData) });\n        // Save to real filesystem\n        saveToDisk(entry.path, finalData);\n      }\n      fdTable.delete(fd);\n      regs[2] = 0;\n      return;\n    }\n    case SYS_LSEEK: { // lseek(fd, offset, whence)\n      const fd = a0, offset = a1, whence = a2;\n      const entry = fdTable.get(fd);\n      if (!entry) { regs[2] = -9; return; }\n      if (whence === 0) entry.pos = offset; // SEEK_SET\n      else if (whence === 1) entry.pos += offset; // SEEK_CUR\n      else if (whence === 2) entry.pos = entry.size + offset; // SEEK_END\n      if (entry.pos < 0) entry.pos = 0;\n      regs[2] = entry.pos;\n      return;\n    }\n    case SYS_FSYNC: { regs[2] = 0; return; }\n    case SYS_RENAME: {\n      const oldPath = normalizePath(readStr(a0 >>> 0));\n      const newPath = normalizePath(readStr(a1 >>> 0));\n      const d = fileStore.get(oldPath);\n      if (d) { fileStore.set(newPath, d); fileStore.delete(oldPath); }\n      regs[2] = 0;\n      return;\n    }\n    case SYS_MKDIR: { regs[2] = 0; return; }\n    case SYS_UNLINK: {\n      const p = normalizePath(readStr(a0 >>> 0));\n      fileStore.delete(p);\n      regs[2] = 0;\n      return;\n    }\n    case SYS_TIME: { // time(tloc)\n      const t = Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000);\n      if (a0 !== 0) w32(a0, t);\n      regs[2] = t;\n      return;\n    }\n    case SYS_EXIT: {\n      console.log('DOOM exited with code ' + a0);\n      process.exit(a0);\n    }\n    default: {\n      // Unknown syscall - return 0\n      regs[2] = 0;\n      return;\n    }\n  }\n}\n\nfunction saveToDisk(filePath, data) {\n  // Save files written by doom to the real filesystem\n  // Ensure parent directories exist\n  let realPath;\n  if (filePath.startsWith('tmp/')) {\n    realPath = '/' + filePath;\n  } else if (filePath.startsWith('/')) {\n    realPath = filePath;\n  } else {\n    realPath = path.join(__dirname, filePath);\n  }\n  const dir = path.dirname(realPath);\n  try { fs.mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }); } catch(e) {}\n  try { fs.writeFileSync(realPath, data); } catch(e) {\n    // Try in current dir\n    try { fs.writeFileSync(path.join(__dirname, path.basename(filePath)), data); } catch(e2) {}\n  }\n}\n\n// FPU state\nconst fprBuf = new ArrayBuffer(128); // 32 * 4 bytes for 32 FPRs\nconst fprU32 = new Uint32Array(fprBuf);\nconst fprI32 = new Int32Array(fprBuf);\nconst fprF32 = new Float32Array(fprBuf);\nconst fprDV = new DataView(fprBuf);\nlet fcc = 0; // FPU condition code\n\nfunction getFPRdouble(reg) {\n  // Even register: low word, reg+1: high word\n  const lo = fprU32[reg & 0x1e];\n  const hi = fprU32[(reg & 0x1e) | 1];\n  const buf = Buffer.alloc(8);\n  buf.writeUInt32LE(lo, 0);\n  buf.writeUInt32LE(hi, 4);\n  return buf.readDoubleLE(0);\n}\nfunction setFPRdouble(reg, val) {\n  const buf = Buffer.alloc(8);\n  buf.writeDoubleLE(val, 0);\n  fprU32[reg & 0x1e] = buf.readUInt32LE(0);\n  fprU32[(reg & 0x1e) | 1] = buf.readUInt32LE(4);\n}\n\n// Main interpreter\nfunction run() {\n  initFiles();\n  const entry = loadELF(path.join(__dirname, 'doomgeneric_mips'));\n\n  // GPR: 32 registers, treat as signed 32-bit\n  const R = new Int32Array(32);\n  R[29] = STACK_TOP; // SP\n  R[28] = 0x00475000; // GP (approximate)\n\n  let pc = entry;\n  let hi = 0, lo = 0;\n  let delayPC = -1;\n  let steps = 0;\n  const MAX_STEPS = 5000000000;\n\n  while (steps < MAX_STEPS) {\n    const inst = ru32(pc);\n    if (inst === 0) { // NOP\n      if (delayPC !== -1) { pc = delayPC; delayPC = -1; } else { pc = (pc + 4) >>> 0; }\n      steps++;\n      continue;\n    }\n\n    const op = 0;\n            break;\n          }\n          case 0x19: { // MULTU\n            const a = BigInt(R[rs] >>> 0);\n            const b = BigInt(R[rt] >>> 0);\n            const result = a * b;\n MEM_SIZE) return 0;\n  return memBuf[off];\n}\nfunction w32(addr, val) {\n  const off = (addr - MEM_BASE) >>> 0;\n  if (off + 3 >= MEM_SIZE) return;\n  memBuf.writeInt32LE(val | 0, off);\n}\nfunction w16(addr, val) {\n  const off = (addr - MEM_BASE) >>> 0;\n  if (off + 1 >= MEM_SIZE) return;\n  memBuf.writeInt16LE(val & 0xffff, off);\n}\nfunction w8(addr, val) {\n  const off = (addr - MEM_BASE) >>> 0;\n  if (off >= MEM_SIZE) return;\n  memBuf[off] = val & 0xff;\n}\n\n// Write string to memory\nfunction writeStr(addr, str) {\n  for (let i = 0; i < str.length; i++) w8(addr + i, str.charCodeAt(i));\n  w8(addr + str.length, 0);\n}\n\n// Read null-terminated string from memory\nfunction readStr(addr) {\n  let s = '';\n  for (let i = 0; i < 4096; i++) {\n    const c = ru8(addr + i);\n    if (c === 0) break;\n    s += String.fromCharCode(c);\n  }\n  return s;\n}\n\n// Load ELF\nfunction loadELF(filePath) {\n  const data = fs.readFileSync(filePath);\n  // Verify ELF magic\n  if (data[0] !== 0x7f || data[1] !== 0x45) throw new Error('Not an ELF file');\n  const entry = data.readUInt32LE(24);\n  const phoff = data.readUInt32LE(28);\n  const phnum = data.readUInt16LE(44);\n  const phentsize = data.readUInt16LE(42);\n\n  for (let i = 0; i < phnum; i++) {\n    const off = phoff + i * phentsize;\n    const type = data.readUInt32LE(off);\n    if (type !== 1) continue; // PT_LOAD\n    const p_offset = data.readUInt32LE(off + 4);\n    const p_vaddr = data.readUInt32LE(off + 8);\n    const p_filesz = data.readUInt32LE(off + 16);\n    const p_memsz = data.readUInt32LE(off + 20);\n\n    // Copy file data\n    const dstOff = (p_vaddr - MEM_BASE) >>> 0;\n    if (dstOff + p_filesz <= MEM_SIZE) {\n      data.copy(memBuf, dstOff, p_offset, p_offset + p_filesz);\n    }\n    // BSS (p_memsz > p_filesz) is already zeroed\n  }\n  return entry;\n}\n\n// File descriptor table for syscalls\nconst fdTable = new Map();\nlet nextFd = 10;\n\n// In-memory file storage\nconst fileStore = new Map(); // path -> {data: Buffer, pos: 0}\n\n// Initialize: load doom.wad into file store\nfunction initFiles() {\n  const wadPath = path.join(__dirname, 'doom.wad');\n  if (fs.existsSync(wadPath)) {\n    fileStore.set('doom.wad', { data: fs.readFileSync(wadPath) });\n    // Also accessible as /doom.wad, ./doom.wad, etc.\n  }\n}\n\nfunction normalizePath(p) {\n  // Strip leading ./ or /\n  p = p.replace(/^\\\\.\\\\//g, '').replace(/^\\\\/+/g, '');\n  return p;\n}\n\n// Syscall numbers (custom, from source)\nconst SYS_READ = 0, SYS_WRITE = 1, SYS_OPEN = 2, SYS_CLOSE = 3;\nconst SYS_LSEEK = 8, SYS_FSYNC = 74, SYS_EXIT = 60;\nconst SYS_RENAME = 82, SYS_MKDIR = 83, SYS_UNLINK = 87;\nconst SYS_TIME = 201;\n\n// Open file flags\nconst O_RDONLY = 0, O_WRONLY = 1, O_RDWR = 2;\nconst O_CREAT = 64, O_TRUNC = 512, O_APPEND = 1024;\n\nfunction handleSyscall(regs) {\n  const num = regs[2]; // $v0\n  const a0 = regs[4], a1 = regs[5], a2 = regs[6], a3 = regs[7];\n\n  switch (num) {\n    case SYS_READ: { // read(fd, buf, count)\n      const fd = a0, bufAddr = a1 >>> 0, count = a2 >>> 0;\n      const entry = fdTable.get(fd);\n      if (!entry) { regs[2] = -9; return; } // EBADF\n      const avail = entry.data.length - entry.pos;\n      const toRead = Math.min(count, avail > 0 ? avail : 0);\n      for (let i = 0; i < toRead; i++) {\n        w8(bufAddr + i, entry.data[entry.pos + i]);\n      }\n      entry.pos += toRead;\n      regs[2] = toRead;\n      return;\n    }\n    case SYS_WRITE: { // write(fd, buf, count)\n      const fd = a0, bufAddr = a1 >>> 0, count = a2 >>> 0;\n      if (fd === 0 || fd === 1 || fd === 2) {\n        // stdout/stderr\n        let s = '';\n        for (let i = 0; i < count; i++) s += String.fromCharCode(ru8(bufAddr + i));\n        process.stdout.write(s);\n        regs[2] = count;\n        return;\n      }\n      const entry = fdTable.get(fd);\n      if (!entry) { regs[2] = -9; return; }\n      // Ensure buffer is large enough\n      const needed = entry.pos + count;\n      if (needed > entry.data.length) {\n        const newBuf = Buffer.alloc(needed);\n        entry.data.copy(newBuf, 0, 0, entry.data.length);\n        entry.data = newBuf;\n      }\n      for (let i = 0; i < count; i++) {\n        entry.data[entry.pos + i] = ru8(bufAddr + i);\n      }\n      entry.pos += count;\n      if (entry.pos > entry.size) entry.size = entry.pos;\n      regs[2] = count;\n      return;\n    }\n    case SYS_OPEN: { // open(path, flags, mode)\n      const pathStr = readStr(a0 >>> 0);\n      const flags = a1;\n      const norm = normalizePath(pathStr);\n\n      if (flags & O_CREAT || flags & O_WRONLY || flags & O_RDWR) {\n        // Creating/writing a file\n        const fd = nextFd++;\n        let existing = fileStore.get(norm);\n        if (existing && !(flags & O_TRUNC)) {\n          fdTable.set(fd, { data: Buffer.from(existing.data), pos: (flags & O_APPEND) ? existing.data.length : 0, size: existing.data.length, path: norm, writable: true });\n        } else {\n          fdTable.set(fd, { data: Buffer.alloc(1024 * 1024 * 2), pos: 0, size: 0, path: norm, writable: true });\n        }\n        regs[2] = fd;\n        return;\n      }\n\n      // Reading\n      const stored = fileStore.get(norm);\n      if (!stored) {\n        regs[2] = -2; // ENOENT\n        return;\n      }\n      const fd = nextFd++;\n      fdTable.set(fd, { data: stored.data, pos: 0, size: stored.data.length, path: norm, writable: false });\n      regs[2] = fd;\n      return;\n    }\n    case SYS_CLOSE: { // close(fd)\n      const fd = a0;\n      const entry = fdTable.get(fd);\n      if (!entry) { regs[2] = -9; return; }\n      // If writable, save to fileStore and possibly to disk\n      if (entry.writable && entry.size > 0) {\n        const finalData = Buffer.from(entry.data.buffer, entry.data.byteOffset, entry.size);\n        fileStore.set(entry.path, { data: Buffer.from(finalData) });\n        // Save to real filesystem\n        saveToDisk(entry.path, finalData);\n      }\n      fdTable.delete(fd);\n      regs[2] = 0;\n      return;\n    }\n    case SYS_LSEEK: { // lseek(fd, offset, whence)\n      const fd = a0, offset = a1, whence = a2;\n      const entry = fdTable.get(fd);\n      if (!entry) { regs[2] = -9; return; }\n      if (whence === 0) entry.pos = offset; // SEEK_SET\n      else if (whence === 1) entry.pos += offset; // SEEK_CUR\n      else if (whence === 2) entry.pos = entry.size + offset; // SEEK_END\n      if (entry.pos < 0) entry.pos = 0;\n      regs[2] = entry.pos;\n      return;\n    }\n    case SYS_FSYNC: { regs[2] = 0; return; }\n    case SYS_RENAME: {\n      const oldPath = normalizePath(readStr(a0 >>> 0));\n      const newPath = normalizePath(readStr(a1 >>> 0));\n      const d = fileStore.get(oldPath);\n      if (d) { fileStore.set(newPath, d); fileStore.delete(oldPath); }\n      regs[2] = 0;\n      return;\n    }\n    case SYS_MKDIR: { regs[2] = 0; return; }\n    case SYS_UNLINK: {\n      const p = normalizePath(readStr(a0 >>> 0));\n      fileStore.delete(p);\n      regs[2] = 0;\n      return;\n    }\n    case SYS_TIME: { // time(tloc)\n      const t = Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000);\n      if (a0 !== 0) w32(a0, t);\n      regs[2] = t;\n      return;\n    }\n    case SYS_EXIT: {\n      console.log('DOOM exited with code ' + a0);\n      process.exit(a0);\n    }\n    default: {\n      // Unknown syscall - return 0\n      regs[2] = 0;\n      return;\n    }\n  }\n}\n\nfunction saveToDisk(filePath, data) {\n  // Save files written by doom to the real filesystem\n  // Ensure parent directories exist\n  let realPath;\n  if (filePath.startsWith('tmp/')) {\n    realPath = '/' + filePath;\n  } else if (filePath.startsWith('/')) {\n    realPath = filePath;\n  } else {\n    realPath = path.join(__dirname, filePath);\n  }\n  const dir = path.dirname(realPath);\n  try { fs.mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }); } catch(e) {}\n  try { fs.writeFileSync(realPath, data); } catch(e) {\n    // Try in current dir\n    try { fs.writeFileSync(path.join(__dirname, path.basename(filePath)), data); } catch(e2) {}\n  }\n}\n\n// FPU state\nconst fprBuf = new ArrayBuffer(128); // 32 * 4 bytes for 32 FPRs\nconst fprU32 = new Uint32Array(fprBuf);\nconst fprI32 = new Int32Array(fprBuf);\nconst fprF32 = new Float32Array(fprBuf);\nconst fprDV = new DataView(fprBuf);\nlet fcc = 0; // FPU condition code\n\nfunction getFPRdouble(reg) {\n  // Even register: low word, reg+1: high word\n  const lo = fprU32[reg & 0x1e];\n  const hi = fprU32[(reg & 0x1e) | 1];\n  const buf = Buffer.alloc(8);\n  buf.writeUInt32LE(lo, 0);\n  buf.writeUInt32LE(hi, 4);\n  return buf.readDoubleLE(0);\n}\nfunction setFPRdouble(reg, val) {\n  const buf = Buffer.alloc(8);\n  buf.writeDoubleLE(val, 0);\n  fprU32[reg & 0x1e] = buf.readUInt32LE(0);\n  fprU32[(reg & 0x1e) | 1] = buf.readUInt32LE(4);\n}\n\n// Main interpreter\nfunction run() {\n  initFiles();\n  const entry = loadELF(path.join(__dirname, 'doomgeneric_mips'));\n\n  // GPR: 32 registers, treat as signed 32-bit\n  const R = new Int32Array(32);\n  R[29] = STACK_TOP; // SP\n  R[28] = 0x00475000; // GP (approximate)\n\n  let pc = entry;\n  let hi = 0, lo = 0;\n  let delayPC = -1;\n  let steps = 0;\n  const MAX_STEPS = 5000000000;\n\n  while (steps < MAX_STEPS) {\n    const inst = ru32(pc);\n    if (inst === 0) { // NOP\n      if (delayPC !== -1) { pc = delayPC; delayPC = -1; } else { pc = (pc + 4) >>> 0; }\n      steps++;\n      continue;\n    }\n\n    const op = inst >>> 26;\n    let branchTarget = -1;\n\n    switch (op) {\n      case 0x00: { // SPECIAL\n        const funct = inst & 0x3f;\n        const rs = (inst >>> 21) & 0x1f;\n        const rt = (inst >>> 16) & 0x1f;\n        const rd = (inst >>> 11) & 0x1f;\n        const sa = (inst >>> 6) & 0x1f;\n        switch (funct) {\n          case 0x00: // SLL\n            R[rd] = (R[rt] << sa) | 0; break;\n          case 0x02: // SRL / ROTR\n            if (rs & 1) { // ROTR\n              R[rd] = ((R[rt] >>> sa) | (R[rt] << (32 - sa))) | 0;\n            } else {\n              R[rd] = (R[rt] >>> sa) | 0;\n            }\n            break;\n          case 0x03: // SRA\n            R[rd] = (R[rt] >> sa) | 0; break;\n          case 0x04: // SLLV\n            R[rd] = (R[rt] << (R[rs] & 0x1f)) | 0; break;\n          case 0x06: // SRLV / ROTRV\n            if (sa & 1) { // ROTRV\n              const sh = R[rs] & 0x1f;\n              R[rd] = ((R[rt] >>> sh) | (R[rt] << (32 - sh))) | 0;\n            } else {\n              R[rd] = (R[rt] >>> (R[rs] & 0x1f)) | 0;\n            }\n            break;\n          case 0x07: // SRAV\n            R[rd] = (R[rt] >> (R[rs] & 0x1f)) | 0; break;\n          case 0x08: // JR\n            branchTarget = R[rs] >>> 0; break;\n          case 0x09: // JALR\n            branchTarget = R[rs] >>> 0;\n            R[rd] = (pc + 8) | 0;\n            break;\n          case 0x0a: // MOVZ\n            if (R[rt] === 0) R[rd] = R[rs]; break;\n          case 0x0b: // MOVN\n            if (R[rt] !== 0) R[rd] = R[rs]; break;\n          case 0x0c: // SYSCALL\n            handleSyscall(R);\n            break;\n          case 0x0d: // BREAK\n            break;\n          case 0x10: // MFHI\n            R[rd] = hi | 0; break;\n          case 0x11: // MTHI\n            hi = R[rs]; break;\n          case 0x12: // MFLO\n            R[rd] = lo | 0; break;\n          case 0x13: // MTLO\n            lo = R[rs]; break;\n          case 0x18: { // MULT\n            const a = BigInt(R[rs]);\n            const b = BigInt(R[rt]);\n            const result = a * b;\n            lo = Number(result & 0xFFFFFFFFn) | 0;\n            hi = Number((result >> 32n) & 0xFFFFFFFFn) | 0;\n            break;\n          }\n          case 0x19: { // MULTU\n            const a = BigInt(R[rs] >>> 0);\n            const b = BigInt(R[rt] >>> 0);\n            const result = a * b;\n            lo = Number(result & 0xFFFFFFFFn) | 0;\n            hi = Number((result >> 32n) & 0xFFFFFFFFn) | 0;\n            break;\n          }\n          case 0x1a: { // DIV\n            if (R[rt] !== 0) {\n              lo = (R[rs] / R[rt]) | 0;\n              hi = (R[rs] % R[rt]) | 0;\n            }\n            break;\n          }\n          case 0x1b: { // DIVU\n            const a = R[rs] >>> 0, b = R[rt] >>> 0;\n            if (b !== 0) {\n              lo = (a / b) >>> 0;\n              hi = (a % b) >>> 0;\n            }\n            break;\n          }\n          case 0x20: // ADD\n            R[rd] = (R[rs] + R[rt]) | 0; break;\n          case 0x21: // ADDU\n            R[rd] = (R[rs] + R[rt]) | 0; break;\n          case 0x22: // SUB\n            R[rd] = (R[rs] - R[rt]) | 0; break;\n          case 0x23: // SUBU\n            R[rd] = (R[rs] - R[rt]) | 0; break;\n          case 0x24: // AND\n            R[rd] = R[rs] & R[rt]; break;\n          case 0x25: // OR\n            R[rd] = R[rs] | R[rt]; break;\n          case 0x26: // XOR\n            R[rd] = R[rs] ^ R[rt]; break;\n          case 0x27: // NOR\n            R[rd] = ~(R[rs] | R[rt]); break;\n          case 0x2a: // SLT\n            R[rd] = (R[rs] < R[rt]) ? 1 : 0; break;\n          case 0x2b: // SLTU\n            R[rd] = ((R[rs] >>> 0) < (R[rt] >>> 0)) ? 1 : 0; break;\n          case 0x34: // TEQ\n            // Trap if equal - for divide by zero checks, just ignore\n            break;\n          default:\n            console.error('Unknown SPECIAL funct: 0x' + funct.toString(16) + ' at PC=0x' + pc.toString(16));\n            break;\n        }\n        break;\n      }\n      case 0x01: { // REGIMM\n        const rt = (inst >>> 16) & 0x1f;\n        const rs = (inst >>> 21) & 0x1f;\n        const imm = (inst << 16) >> 14; // sign-extended, *4\n        switch (rt) {\n          case 0x00: // BLTZ\n            if (R[rs] < 0) branchTarget = ((pc + 4) + imm) >>> 0;\n            break;\n          case 0x01: // BGEZ\n            if (R[rs] >= 0) branchTarget = ((pc + 4) + imm) >>> 0;\n            break;\n          case 0x10: // BLTZAL\n            R[31] = (pc + 8) | 0;\n            if (R[rs] < 0) branchTarget = ((pc + 4) + imm) >>> 0;\n            break;\n          case 0x11: // BGEZAL\n            R[31] = (pc + 8) | 0;\n            if (R[rs] >= 0) branchTarget = ((pc + 4) + imm) >>> 0;\n            break;\n        }\n        break;\n      }\n      case 0x02: // J\n        branchTarget = (((pc + 4) & 0xf0000000) | ((inst & 0x03ffffff) << 2)) >>> 0;\n        break;\n      case 0x03: // JAL\n        R[31] = (pc + 8) | 0;\n        branchTarget = (((pc + 4) & 0xf0000000) | ((inst & 0x03ffffff) << 2)) >>> 0;\n        break;\n      case 0x04: { // BEQ\n        const rs = (inst >>> 21) & 0x1f, rt = (inst >>> 16) & 0x1f;\n        const imm = (inst << 16) >> 14;\n        if (R[rs] === R[rt]) branchTarget = ((pc + 4) + imm) >>> 0;\n        break;\n      }\n      case 0x05: { // BNE\n        const rs = (inst >>> 21) & 0x1f, rt = (inst >>> 16) & 0x1f;\n        const imm = (inst << 16) >> 14;\n        if (R[rs] !== R[rt]) branchTarget = ((pc + 4) + imm) >>> 0;\n        break;\n      }\n      case 0x06: { // BLEZ\n        const rs = (inst >>> 21) & 0x1f;\n        const imm = (inst << 16) >> 14;\n        if (R[rs] <= 0) branchTarget = ((pc + 4) + imm) >>> 0;\n        break;\n      }\n      case 0x07: { // BGTZ\n        const rs = (inst >>> 21) & 0x1f;\n        const imm = (inst << 16) >> 14;\n        if (R[rs] > 0) branchTarget = ((pc + 4) + imm) >>> 0;\n        break;\n      }\n      case 0x08: { // ADDI\n        const rs = (inst >>> 21) & 0x1f, rt = (inst >>> 16) & 0x1f;\n        const imm = (inst << 16) >> 16;\n        R[rt] = (R[rs] + imm) | 0;\n        break;\n      }\n      case 0x09: { // ADDIU\n        const rs = (inst >>> 21) & 0x1f, rt = (inst >>> 16) & 0x1f;\n        const imm = (inst << 16) >> 16;\n        R[rt] = (R[rs] + imm) | 0;\n        break;\n      }\n      case 0x0a: { // SLTI\n        const rs = (inst >>> 21) & 0x1f, rt = (inst >>> 16) & 0x1f;\n        const imm = (inst << 16) >> 16;\n        R[rt] = (R[rs] < imm) ? 1 : 0;\n        break;\n      }\n      case 0x0b: { // SLTIU\n        const rs = (inst >>> 21) & 0x1f, rt = (inst >>> 16) & 0x1f;\n        const imm = (inst << 16) >> 16;\n        R[rt] = ((R[rs] >>> 0) < (imm >>> 0)) ? 1 : 0;\n        break;\n      }\n      case 0x0c: { // ANDI\n        const rs = (inst >>> 21) & 0x1f, rt = (inst >>> 16) & 0x1f;\n        const imm = inst & 0xffff;\n        R[rt] = R[rs] & imm;\n        break;\n      }\n      case 0x0d: { // ORI\n        const rs = (inst >>> 21) & 0x1f, rt = (inst >>> 16) & 0x1f;\n        const imm = inst & 0xffff;\n        R[rt] = R[rs] | imm;\n        break;\n      }\n      case 0x0e: { // XORI\n        const rs = (inst >>> 21) & 0x1f, rt = (inst >>> 16) & 0x1f;\n        const imm = inst & 0xffff;\n        R[rt] = R[rs] ^ imm;\n        break;\n      }\n      case 0x0f: { // LUI\n        const rt = (inst >>> 16) & 0x1f;\n        const imm = inst & 0xffff;\n        R[rt] = (imm << 16) | 0;\n        break;\n      }\n      case 0x1c: { // SPECIAL2\n        const funct = inst & 0x3f;\n        const rs = (inst >>> 21) & 0x1f;\n        const rt = (inst >>> 16) & 0x1f;\n        const rd = (inst >>> 11) & 0x1f;\n        switch (funct) {\n          case 0x02: // MUL\n            R[rd] = Math.imul(R[rs], R[rt]); break;\n          case 0x20: // CLZ\n            R[rd] = R[rs] === 0 ? 32 : Math.clz32(R[rs] >>> 0); break;\n          case 0x21: // CLO\n            R[rd] = Math.clz32(~R[rs] >>> 0); break;\n          default: break;\n        }\n        break;\n      }\n      case 0x1f: { // SPECIAL3\n        const funct = inst & 0x3f;\n        const rs = (inst >>> 21) & 0x1f;\n        const rt = (inst >>> 16) & 0x1f;\n        const rd = (inst >>> 11) & 0x1f;\n        const sa = (inst >>> 6) & 0x1f;\n        switch (funct) {\n          case 0x00: { // EXT - extract bit field\n            const pos = sa;\n            const size = rd + 1;\n            R[rt] = (R[rs] >>> pos) & ((1 << size) - 1);\n            break;\n          }\n          case 0x04: { // INS - insert bit field\n            const pos = sa;\n            const size = rd - pos + 1;\n            const mask = ((1 << size) - 1) << pos;\n            R[rt] = (R[rt] & ~mask) | ((R[rs] << pos) & mask);\n            break;\n          }\n          case 0x20: { // BSHFL\n            switch (sa) {\n              case 0x02: // WSBH - word swap bytes within halfwords\n                R[rd] = (((R[rt] & 0xff00ff00) >>> 8) | ((R[rt] & 0x00ff00ff) << 8)) | 0;\n                break;\n              case 0x10: // SEB - sign extend byte\n                R[rd] = (R[rt] << 24) >> 24; break;\n              case 0x18: // SEH - sign extend halfword\n                R[rd] = (R[rt] << 16) >> 16; break;\n            }\n            break;\n          }\n          default: break;\n        }\n        break;\n      }\n      case 0x11: { // COP1 (FPU)\n        const fmt = (inst >>> 21) & 0x1f;\n        const ft = (inst >>> 16) & 0x1f;\n        const fs_ = (inst >>> 11) & 0x1f;\n        const fd = (inst >>> 6) & 0x1f;\n        const cfunct = inst & 0x3f;\n        switch (fmt) {\n          case 0x00: // MFC1\n            R[ft] = fprI32[fs_]; break;\n          case 0x04: // MTC1\n            fprI32[fs_] = R[ft]; break;\n          case 0x03: // MFHC1\n            R[ft] = fprI32[fs_ | 1]; break;\n          case 0x07: // MTHC1\n            fprI32[fs_ | 1] = R[ft]; break;\n          case 0x08: // BC1\n            if (ft & 1) { // BC1T\n              if (fcc) branchTarget = ((pc + 4) + ((inst << 16) >> 14)) >>> 0;\n            } else { // BC1F\n              if (!fcc) branchTarget = ((pc + 4) + ((inst << 16) >> 14)) >>> 0;\n            }\n            break;\n          case 0x10: { // S (single precision)\n            const a = fprF32[fs_], b = fprF32[ft];\n            switch (cfunct) {\n              case 0x00: fprF32[fd] = a + b; break; // ADD.S\n              case 0x01: fprF32[fd] = a - b; break; // SUB.S\n              case 0x02: fprF32[fd] = a * b; break; // MUL.S\n              case 0x03: fprF32[fd] = a / b; break; // DIV.S\n              case 0x06: fprF32[fd] = a; break; // MOV.S\n              case 0x07: fprF32[fd] = -a; break; // NEG.S\n              case 0x0d: fprI32[fd] = Math.trunc(a); break; // TRUNC.W.S\n              case 0x21: setFPRdouble(fd, a); break; // CVT.D.S\n              case 0x11: if (fcc) fprF32[fd] = fprF32[fs_]; break; // MOVT.S\n              case 0x34: fcc = (a < b) ? 1 : 0; break; // C.OLT.S\n              default: break;\n            }\n            break;\n          }\n          case 0x11: { // D (double precision)\n            const a = getFPRdouble(fs_), b = getFPRdouble(ft);\n            switch (cfunct) {\n              case 0x00: setFPRdouble(fd, a + b); break; // ADD.D\n              case 0x01: setFPRdouble(fd, a - b); break; // SUB.D\n              case 0x02: setFPRdouble(fd, a * b); break; // MUL.D\n              case 0x03: setFPRdouble(fd, a / b); break; // DIV.D\n              case 0x06: setFPRdouble(fd, a); break; // MOV.D\n              case 0x07: setFPRdouble(fd, -a); break; // NEG.D\n              case 0x11: if (fcc) setFPRdouble(fd, getFPRdouble(fs_)); break; // MOVT.D\n              case 0x34: fcc = (a < b) ? 1 : 0; break; // C.OLT.D\n              default: break;\n            }\n            break;\n          }\n          case 0x14: { // W (word - integer in FPR)\n            switch (cfunct) {\n              case 0x20: fprF32[fd] = fprI32[fs_]; break; // CVT.S.W\n              case 0x21: setFPRdouble(fd, fprI32[fs_]); break; // CVT.D.W\n              default: break;\n            }\n            break;\n          }\n          default: break;\n        }\n        break;\n      }\n      case 0x20: { // LB\n        const rs = (inst >>> 21) & 0x1f, rt = (inst >>> 16) & 0x1f;\n        const imm = (inst << 16) >> 16;\n        R[rt] = r8((R[rs] + imm) >>> 0);\n        break;\n      }\n      case 0x21: { // LH\n        const rs = (inst >>> 21) & 0x1f, rt = (inst >>> 16) & 0x1f;\n        const imm = (inst << 16) >> 16;\n        R[rt] = r16((R[rs] + imm) >>> 0);\n        break;\n      }\n      case 0x22: { // LWL\n        const rs = (inst >>> 21) & 0x1f, rt = (inst >>> 16) & 0x1f;\n        const >>> 0, R[rt]);\n        break;\n      }\n      case 0x29: { // SH\n        const rs = (inst >>> 21) & 0x1f, rt = (inst >>> 16) & 0x1f;\n        const imm = (inst << 16) >> 16;\n        w16((R[rs] + imm) >>> 0, R[rt]);\n        break;\n      }\n      case 0x2a: { // SWL\n        const rs = (inst >>> 21) & 0x1f, rt = (inst >>> 16) & 0x1f;\n        const imm = (inst << 16) >> 16;\n        const r32(addr) {\n  const off = (addr - MEM_BASE) >>> 0;\n  if (off + 3 >= MEM_SIZE) return 0;\n  return memBuf.readInt32LE(off);\n}\nfunction ru32(addr) {\n  const off = (addr - MEM_BASE) >>> 0;\n  if (off + 3 >= MEM_SIZE) return 0;\n  return memBuf.readUInt32LE(off);\n}\nfunction r16(addr) {\n  const off = (addr - MEM_BASE) >>> 0;\n  if (off + 1 >= MEM_SIZE) return 0;\n  return memBuf.readInt16LE(off);\n}\nfunction ru16(addr) {\n  const off = (addr - MEM_BASE) >>> 0;\n  if (off + 1 >= MEM_SIZE) return 0;\n  return memBuf.readUInt16LE(off);\n}\nfunction r8(addr) {\n  const MEM_SIZE) return 0;\n  return memBuf[off];\n}\nfunction w32(addr, val) {\n  const off = (addr - MEM_BASE) >>> 0;\n  if (off + 3 >= MEM_SIZE) return;\n  memBuf.writeInt32LE(val | 0, off);\n}\nfunction
